executable_validators_to_add.pass: All required concept artifacts exist and include intent, references, shape language, material palette, gameplay readability notes, and asset prompt notes.
Every production area has lifecycle, source policy, route clearance, collision policy, validation cameras, screenshot score fields, and blocked items.
Every old non-Kenney visible asset has a replacement, rejection, or keep-review decision with validation result.
No final visible stage prop, wall dressing, window, door, furniture item, street detail, yard object, or route landmark is an uncovered Godot primitive.
Route corridors stay inside zones, above floor/ground, clear of unplanned collision, and maintain configured lateral and vertical clearances.

Every public course instantiates through the runtime builder and proves wall, furniture, prop, shell, and imported asset AABBs stay outside the route corridor, spawn grid, drift margin, and third-person chase-camera swept volume unless declared as intentional named hazards.

Each public course has start_grid, third_person_launch, first_turn_chase, camera_clearance, overhead_route, and route_sample screenshots with critical review scores proving the road, next turn, and chase camera are not blocked.
Exterior windows use transparent or glass-readable materials and close-up cameras validate no opaque patches or void leaks.
Generated visible nodes are represented by generator metadata, mode metadata, area manifests, or replacement provenance.
Triangle, material, texture, and import-compression budgets stay inside the manifest target profile.
Every required camera has a critical designer score record and no category is below the production threshold.
The import recovery helper contract covers Reload from disk, Load Errors OK, editor readiness, camera list, and camera-current validation.
